title: Tensions Rise as Iran Threatens to Close Vital Shipping Route
subtitle: A new peace deal between the United States and Iran faces a difficult test following military actions in Lebanon.

summary.en: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to recent Israeli military strikes in Lebanon. This move threatens a fragile 60-day ceasefire agreement signed between the US and Iran just days ago. While Iran claims the waterway is closed, the US military insists that traffic is still moving normally. International leaders are now heading to Switzerland to continue peace talks despite the rising violence. The situation remains uncertain as both sides struggle to maintain the new peace deal.
summary.zh: 伊朗宣布封鎖荷莫茲海峽，以回應以色列近期對黎巴嫩的軍事打擊。此舉威脅到美國與伊朗幾天前才剛簽署的六十天脆弱停火協議。儘管伊朗聲稱該水道已封鎖，但美軍堅稱交通仍正常運行。儘管暴力事件升溫，國際領袖仍正前往瑞士繼續進行和平談判。由於雙方皆難以維持這項新和平協議，局勢仍充滿不確定性。

paragraphs.en: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway that carries about one-fifth of the world’s oil and gas supplies, has become the center of a new international crisis. Iran recently announced that it is closing the strait, citing recent Israeli military strikes in Lebanon. This decision directly challenges a fragile interim peace deal—a temporary agreement to stop fighting—that was signed by the United States and Iran only days ago.
paragraphs.zh: 荷莫茲海峽是全球約五分之一石油與天然氣供應的關鍵水道，如今已成為一場新國際危機的中心。伊朗近期宣布封鎖該海峽，理由是以色列近期對黎巴嫩發動軍事打擊。這項決定直接挑戰了美國與伊朗幾天前才剛簽署的一項脆弱臨時和平協議，即一項旨在停止戰鬥的暫時性協定。

paragraphs.en: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a warning to ships, telling them not to approach the area. They stated that this move was a response to what they described as Israeli crimes in Lebanon and a failure by the United States to uphold its promises regarding a ceasefire. A ceasefire is an agreement to stop fighting for a set period of time. It is currently unclear if Iran has actually stopped ships from passing through or if this is a warning intended to put pressure on the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗伊斯蘭革命衛隊對船隻發出警告，要求它們不得靠近該區域。他們聲稱此舉是為了回應他們所謂以色列在黎巴嫩犯下的罪行，以及美國未能履行關於停火的承諾。停火是指在一段特定時間內停止戰鬥的協議。目前尚不清楚伊朗是否真的阻止了船隻通行，還是這僅是為了對美國施壓而發出的警告。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the United States military has denied that the strait is closed. Navy Captain Tim Hawkins, a spokesperson for US Central Command, stated that traffic continues to flow through the area. He added that US forces are watching the situation closely to ensure that the waterway remains open, as required by the new agreement between the two nations. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has publicly stated that no tolls or fees will be charged to ships passing through the strait during the 60-day ceasefire period. However, he also suggested that the US might consider charging a toll if future negotiations fail.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》報導，美國軍方否認海峽遭到封鎖。美國中央司令部發言人、海軍上校提姆·霍金斯（Tim Hawkins）表示，該區域的交通持續暢通。他補充說，美軍正密切關注局勢，以確保該水道維持開放，正如兩國新協議所要求的那樣。與此同時，川普（Donald Trump）公開表示，在六十天的停火期間，通過該海峽的船隻將不會被收取任何通行費或費用。然而，他也暗示若未來的談判失敗，美國可能會考慮收取費用。

paragraphs.en: The tension comes at a difficult time as officials prepare for important meetings in Switzerland. These talks were intended to turn the current temporary agreement into a more detailed plan regarding Iran’s nuclear program. Despite the threats, Pakistan—which is acting as a mediator—reports that the talks will continue as planned. A group of senior Iranian officials has reportedly left Tehran to attend the meetings. US Vice-President JD Vance is also traveling to Switzerland to participate, expressing hope that the discussions will lead to progress on both the nuclear issue and the situation in Lebanon.
paragraphs.zh: 在官員們準備前往瑞士參加重要會議之際，緊張局勢正處於艱難時刻。這些會談原定將目前的臨時協議轉化為關於伊朗核計畫更詳細的方案。儘管面臨威脅，擔任調解人的巴基斯坦表示，會談將按計畫進行。據報導，一群伊朗資深官員已離開德黑蘭前往參加會議。美國副總統范斯（JD Vance）也正前往瑞士參與，並表示希望討論能針對核問題與黎巴嫩局勢取得進展。

paragraphs.en: The conflict in Lebanon remains the biggest challenge to the new peace deal. According to The Guardian, recent Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of at least 16 people. Local civil defense workers reported that they had been working since the early morning to transport the dead and wounded to hospitals. The Israeli military stated that these strikes were a response to projectile launches from Hezbollah, a militant organization with close ties to Iran. This violence follows a series of clashes that killed four Israeli soldiers, leading to a wider wave of attacks that reportedly killed 83 people across southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley.
paragraphs.zh: 黎巴嫩的衝突仍是這項新和平協議面臨的最大挑戰。根據《衛報》報導，以色列近期在黎巴嫩南部的打擊造成至少十六人死亡。當地民防人員表示，他們從清晨開始就一直在運送死傷者至醫院。以色列軍方表示，這些打擊是為了回應來自真主黨（Hezbollah）的飛彈發射，這是一個與伊朗關係密切的武裝組織。這波暴力事件發生在一連串衝突之後，先前衝突造成四名以色列士兵死亡，隨後引發了更廣泛的攻擊浪潮，據報導在黎巴嫩南部與貝卡谷地造成八十三人死亡。

paragraphs.en: The status of the local ceasefire, which was supposed to begin on Friday evening, remains unclear. While Hezbollah has stated in public that it is willing to follow a ceasefire if Israel does the same, the group has not confirmed that one is currently active. The interim agreement has also faced strong criticism from some Israeli officials. These critics argue that the deal prevents Israel from properly defending itself against threats from Hezbollah.
paragraphs.zh: 原定於週五晚間生效的當地停火協議，其狀態仍不明朗。雖然真主黨公開表示若以色列採取相同行動，他們願意遵守停火，但該組織並未證實目前已有停火協議生效。這項臨時協議也面臨部分以色列官員的強烈批評。這些批評者認為，該協議阻礙了以色列針對真主黨的威脅進行適當的自我防衛。

paragraphs.en: As the situation develops, the world is watching to see if the agreement can survive. The success of the upcoming talks in Switzerland will likely depend on whether both sides can lower the level of violence and return to the terms of their initial deal. For now, the global community remains concerned about the stability of the region and the safety of one of the world’s most important shipping routes.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著局勢發展，全球都在關注這項協議能否存續。即將在瑞士舉行的會談是否成功，很可能取決於雙方能否降低暴力程度並回歸最初協議的條款。目前，國際社會仍對該地區的穩定性以及全球最重要航運路線之一的安全感到擔憂。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To maintain or support a promise or principle.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常與法律或原則連用，這裡指維持承諾。文中：a failure by the United States to uphold its promises regarding a ceasefire.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The movement of ships, vehicles, or people through a specific area.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （船隻、車輛或人員的）往來、通行。
keyTerms.noteZh: 大眾常將此詞與「交通堵塞」連結，這裡指船隻的航行往來。文中：Navy Captain Tim Hawkins, a spokesperson for US Central Command, stated that traffic continues to flow through the area.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To change or transform something into something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「轉彎」，這裡指將某事物轉化為另一種狀態。文中：These talks were intended to turn the current temporary agreement into a more detailed plan regarding Iran’s nuclear program.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Iran says it is closing strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon

previousArticles.title: Tensions Rise as Iran Claims Closure of Strategic Strait of Hormuz
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, citing recent Israeli military actions in Lebanon. This move challenges a new interim peace deal between Iran and the United States. While Iran claims the waterway is closed, the US military reports that commercial traffic continues to flow. US Vice-President JD Vance is traveling to Switzerland for high-level talks to address the nuclear issue and the ceasefire. International mediators from Pakistan are working to keep the negotiations on track despite the ongoing violence.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Sign Initial Agreement to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump has signed an initial agreement with Iran to end their recent war. The deal creates a 60-day ceasefire and plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for shipping. Both nations have committed to future negotiations regarding Iran's nuclear program and economic sanctions. While the US government calls this a major win, the agreement faces criticism from some political groups. A final, permanent peace deal remains the goal for the coming months.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason given by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps for their warning regarding the Strait of Hormuz?

options.A: The need to collect tolls from passing ships.
options.B: Retaliation for Israeli military actions in Lebanon and perceived US failures regarding the ceasefire.
options.C: A direct order from the government in Switzerland to stop all maritime traffic.
options.D: To prevent the US military from accessing the nuclear program discussions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，伊斯蘭革命衛隊明確表示此舉是對以色列在黎巴嫩的「罪行」以及美國未能遵守停火協議承諾的回應。選項 A 錯誤，因為收取通行費是川普提到的未來可能性，而非革命衛隊的動機。選項 C 與文章提到的瑞士會議無關。選項 D 屬於錯誤推論，文中並未指控美國軍隊試圖干預核談判。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the current status of the interim peace deal?

options.A: It has been officially canceled by the United States and Iran.
options.B: It is successfully preventing all violence in the region.
options.C: It is highly unstable and under significant pressure from ongoing regional conflicts.
options.D: It has already led to a permanent solution for Iran's nuclear program.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第一段提到該協議是「脆弱的」(fragile)，且後續段落描述了黎巴嫩的持續衝突、伊朗對海峽的威脅以及以色列官員的批評，這些都顯示協議正處於極度不穩定的狀態。選項 A 錯誤，因為談判仍在進行中。選項 B 顯然與文中提到的暴力事件矛盾。選項 D 錯誤，因為該協議僅是臨時性的，且核問題的詳細計畫仍在討論中。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'mediator' mean in the context of Pakistan's role?

options.A: A neutral party that helps conflicting sides reach an agreement.
options.B: A military ally that provides weapons to one side.
options.C: A reporter who documents the history of the conflict.
options.D: A government official who makes final legal decisions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在外交語境中，mediator 指的是「調解人」或「中間人」，即不偏袒任何一方，協助衝突雙方溝通並達成協議的角色。文中提到巴基斯坦作為調解人，負責確保瑞士的談判如期進行。選項 B 錯誤，因為調解人通常保持中立而非提供軍事援助。選項 C 錯誤，調解人是參與者而非記錄者。選項 D 錯誤，調解人沒有法律上的最終裁決權。

quiz.question: What is the central theme of this article?

options.A: The history of the Strait of Hormuz as a global oil route.
options.B: The specific details of Israel's military strategy against Hezbollah.
options.C: The challenges facing a fragile US-Iran peace deal amid ongoing regional instability.
options.D: The personal involvement of Donald Trump and JD Vance in Swiss diplomatic talks.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於探討美伊之間剛簽署的「臨時停火協議」如何因黎巴嫩衝突、海峽威脅及各方政治壓力而面臨崩潰風險。選項 A 僅是背景資訊，非文章主旨。選項 B 僅是導致局勢緊張的其中一個因素。選項 D 提到的人物僅是事件參與者，而非文章討論的整體議題核心。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
